plugin_desc: D-Link DES/DGS-specific SNMP collection for switches (EQUIPMENT-MIB, enterprise 171). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects D-Link private OIDs for device health: CPU utilization, memory utilization, temperature sensors, fan and power-supply status. D-Link reports CPU and memory as utilization percentages directly; fan status is natively 1=normal and power-supply status is normalized to the unified 1=normal convention. Supported brands/models: D-Link DES / DGS series and DES-7200 (all D-Link enterprise 171; specific health OIDs vary by model).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the D-Link device control plane (EQUIPMENT-MIB swCPUUtilizationIn5min), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the D-Link device (EQUIPMENT-MIB swDevInfoTotalUtilizationOfMemory), reported directly as a 0-100 percentage. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the temperature reading of each D-Link hardware sensor (EQUIPMENT-MIB swTemperatureCurrent), in degrees Celsius. Abnormally high temperatures may indicate fan failure, blocked airflow, or excessive ambient heat, and can trigger thermal protection or hardware damage if left unaddressed.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each D-Link cooling fan (EQUIPMENT-MIB swFanStatus), where 1 means working/normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state indicates a cooling fault that can quickly lead to overheating and should be investigated immediately.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the operational state of each D-Link power supply unit (EQUIPMENT-MIB swPowerStatus), normalized to the unified convention where 1 means normal and any other value indicates an abnormal/fault state. A non-normal state signals a power redundancy or hardware fault and must be remediated promptly.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
